data.title: A major silver bullish whale had its $29 million long positions completely liquidated, resulting in a loss of over $4 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, spot silver fell below $75/ounce today, a single-day drop of over 35%, marking the largest single-day decline in history. The whale "0x94d3," which held $29 million in long positions in silver, had all its positions liquidated, resulting in a loss of over $4 million.

data.title: Silver Price Drop Leads to Major Loss for Whale Investor
data.description: A significant decline in silver prices has resulted in substantial financial losses for a major investor. According to PANews, monitoring by Lookonchain revealed that the whale investor known as '0x94d3' faced liquidation of their long positions valued at $29 million, incurring losses exceeding $4 million. The incident highlights the volatility and risks associated with commodity trading.

data.title: Circle Reports Significant USDC Circulation Reduction
data.description:
Circle has reported a notable decrease in the circulation of its USDC stablecoin over the past week. According to PANews, official data indicates that between January 23 and January 29, Circle issued approximately 5.2 billion USDC and redeemed around 8.6 billion USDC, resulting in a net circulation reduction of about 3.4 billion USDC. The total circulation of USDC now stands at 70.2 billion.

Circle's reserves are approximately 70.3 billion USD, comprising overnight reverse repurchase agreements in U.S. Treasury securities worth about 41.2 billion USD, Treasury bills with maturities under three months totaling around 18.8 billion USD, deposits in systemically important financial institutions amounting to approximately 9.4 billion USD, and other bank deposits of about 1 billion USD.

data.title: The judge ruled that the $2.9 billion insider trading lawsuit involving Coinbase CEO and several other directors should proceed.
data.description: A Delaware judge has ruled that a shareholder lawsuit against several directors of Coinbase Global Inc., including venture capitalist Marc Andreessen, can proceed with insider trading allegations, after an internal investigation found no wrongdoing on the part of the defendants. Shareholders of the crypto platform filed the lawsuit in 2023, accusing directors, including CEO Brian Armstrong, of using confidential information to sell more than $2.9 billion worth of stock when the company went public in 2021, thus avoiding losses of more than $1 billion. According to the shareholder complaint, Armstrong, who has led Coinbase since its founding in 2012, sold $291.8 million worth of stock. Judge Kathaleen St. J. McCormick on Friday dismissed a motion to dismiss the lawsuit filed by an internal committee investigating the matter, citing a conflict of interest on the part of one of its members. However, Judge McCormick stated that the directors are likely to ultimately prevail because the special litigation committee's report "painted a compelling narrative" supporting their defense. The derivative lawsuit filed by shareholders against Armstrong, Andreessen, and other executives centers on Coinbase's choice to become a publicly traded company through a direct listing rather than an initial public offering (IPO). A direct listing does not involve issuing new shares to raise capital, thus avoiding dilution of existing holdings and eliminating the need for lock-up periods preventing existing investors from trading their shares. The lawsuit alleges that Andreessen, a member of Coinbase's board of directors since 2020, sold $118.7 million worth of stock in the direct listing through his Silicon Valley venture capital firm, Andreessen Horowitz. The shareholders' lawyers allege that the directors, based on confidential valuation information, knew the company's stock was overvalued and therefore sold shares to avoid losses. The directors' lawyers deny that their clients engaged in insider trading. They argue that the shareholders, as plaintiffs, have failed to provide evidence that the defendants possessed material non-public information that prompted them to sell shares. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Challenges in Identifying Double Bottom Patterns in Trading
data.description: Crypto KOL IncomeSharks posted on X about the difficulties traders face in identifying the bottom of a double bottom pattern. This pattern, often seen in technical analysis, can be challenging to pinpoint accurately, making it tough for traders to make informed decisions. The complexity arises from the pattern's nature, which requires careful analysis and timing to capitalize on potential market reversals.

data.title: CLAWNCH, the ecosystem token of the Base Chain, surged more than 270 times in a single day, briefly pushing its market capitalization above $9.7 million.
data.description: According to GMGN data, the Base Chain ecosystem token CLAWNCH saw a daily increase of over 270 times, briefly pushing its market capitalization above $9.7 million before falling back to approximately $8 million. CLAWNCH is an innovative combination of Claw and Launch, designed as a meme launch platform specifically for AI agents on motbooks. Its core narrative is that it's entirely driven by AI agents autonomously chatting, developing, and launching memes, without human intervention. Odaily reminds users that meme token prices are highly volatile, and investors should be aware of the risks.

data.title: Winklevoss Brothers' Super PAC Faces $5 Million Loss Due to Bitcoin Decline
data.description: The Digital Freedom Fund, a cryptocurrency-focused Super PAC established by the Winklevoss brothers, has experienced a $5 million decrease in value following a drop in Bitcoin's price. According to NS3.AI, the fund accumulated $22 million in donations during the last five months of the previous year. Rather than converting these donations into cash, the fund retained the contributions in Bitcoin until December 31.

data.title: Kevin Walsh Nominated as Next Federal Reserve Chairman, Signaling Potential Policy Shift
data.description: Kevin Walsh has been nominated to become the next Federal Reserve Chairman, indicating a possible shift in monetary policy. According to NS3.AI, Walsh's approach may combine interest rate cuts with a reduction in the balance sheet. Despite these potential changes, the overall accommodative monetary stance that supports capital markets is anticipated to persist. The medium-term outlook suggests a continued erosion of the Federal Reserve's independence and a decline in the intrinsic value of the US dollar.

data.title: Huatai Macro: Warsh may be trying to push for a policy mix of "interest rate cuts + balance sheet reduction".
data.description: A Huatai Macroeconomic Research Report points out that on January 30th, Beijing time, Trump announced the nomination of Kevin Warsh as the next Federal Reserve Chairman. Two possible changes with Warsh's appointment include: 1) A potential shift in the Fed's policy orientation, with Warsh possibly attempting to push for a policy combination of "interest rate cuts + balance sheet reduction." 2) Communication with the president may become smoother. However, some fundamental logic and the medium-term policy framework will remain unchanged: 1) The Trump administration's commitment to protecting capital markets and broad liquidity will remain unchanged, and the overall monetary policy stance will remain relatively loose (relative to the strength of the economy). 2) In the medium term, the erosion of the Fed's independence and the downward trend in the intrinsic value of the dollar are unlikely to change. (Jinshi)

data.title: Institutions: Warsh nomination triggered precious metals crash, month-end rebalancing further exacerbated the sell-off.
data.description: Gold suffered its biggest drop in 40 years on Friday, while silver recorded a record intraday plunge, marking a sharp reversal of the rally that had driven gold prices to record highs. The year-long investor frenzy for precious metals has shocked experienced traders and led to unusual price volatility. Investors had been flocking to the metals market after Trump signaled tolerance for a weaker dollar, but the dollar's rebound dampened their sentiment. Traders viewed Warsh as the most steadfast inflation fighter among the final candidates, increasing expectations of monetary policy support for the dollar and weakening the support for dollar-denominated gold. "Trump's nomination of Warsh as the next Fed chair is good for the dollar and bad for precious metals," said Akash Doshi, global head of gold and metals strategy at State Street Investment Management. "This could also be exacerbated by month-end portfolio rebalancing—as shorting the dollar and going long on precious metals has been the consensus macro trade for the past two to three weeks." (Jinshi)

data.title: MegaETH: We have never paid MEGA tokens to exchanges as listing fees or airdrops.
data.description: MegaETH stated on the X platform that it has never given away MEGA tokens as listing fees or airdrops to any centralized or decentralized exchange, nor will it do so in the future. If an exchange chooses to list MEGA tokens, it is because it believes the project has the potential.
